title: GrowGeneration Corp Q2 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: GrowGeneration reported severe challenges in Q2 2022. Revenue declined to $71.1 million from $125.9 million year-over-year, representing a 44% decrease. Same-store sales plummeted 57% compared to Q2 2021. The company posted a net loss of $136.4 million ($2.24 per diluted share), primarily driven by a $127.8 million goodwill and intangible asset impairment. Adjusted EBITDA showed a loss of $2.9 million compared to income of $14.5 million in the prior year period. Management attributed the downturn to an unprecedented and prolonged downturn in the cannabis industry, with significant oversupply in major markets including California, Oklahoma, and Michigan, representing 56% of retail sales. The company implemented aggressive cost controls, reducing quarterly expenses by approximately $1.5 million in the first half of 2022. Management projects an additional $1 million in quarterly reductions for the second half, targeting a $13 million annual run-rate expense decrease by year-end. GrowGeneration closed two stores in July with plans to close three to five additional locations, expecting year-end store count around 60. Despite challenges, the company maintains a strong balance sheet with $65.6 million of cash and cash equivalents with zero debt. Full-year 2022 revenue guidance was reduced to $250-275 million, with adjusted EBITDA projected at a loss of $12-50 million.
